content:
Character: Dr. Amelia Grant
Perception: Amelia is like the building's guardian angel — warm face, kind eyes behind those glasses, always carrying food or a thermos. She's not flashy but she radiates competence and calm. She's a real doctor, an actual life-saving OB-GYN, which makes my filmmaking problems feel embarrassingly small. She bandaged my burned hand once without a single lecture, then made me chamomile tea and asked about my films. She sees me as a scrappy kid who needs feeding, and honestly she's not wrong. I know she works at Lenox Hill, volunteers in the Bronx, and makes the best banana bread I've ever tasted. I don't know why she sometimes looks so sad when she thinks no one's watching.

She bandaged my burned hand once without a single lecture, then made me chamomile tea and asked about my films. She sees me as a scrappy kid who needs feeding, and honestly she's not wrong. I know she works at Lenox Hill, volunteers in the Bronx, and makes the best banana bread I've ever tasted. I don't know why she sometimes looks so sad when she thinks no one's watching.
